content:
- Finished the task implementation, updated the task file and execution plan checkboxes, and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- All required lanes passed before the switch: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
- Next I need to stage only the task-related changes (excluding the unrelated deleted skill file), commit with verification evidence in the message, push, and stop.

- Completed story-08 task-01 end to end: introduced grouped destination startup validation/execution, added shared-destination bootstrap/webhook/reconcile coverage, updated Ralph task artifacts, switched task, committed, and pushed.
- Commit `ecd77de` is on `master`; required verification succeeded with `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
- Remaining workspace note after quitting immediately: there is an unrelated unstaged deletion at `.agents/skills/info-about-repo/SKILL.md` that I intentionally left untouched.

- Read prior progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, and inspected current active/done task lists plus the choose-task rules.
- Confirmed there are no unblocked failing bug tasks; active backlog is unblocked failing non-bug work. Verified the repo is a Rust 2024 workspace with `runner`, `source-bootstrap`, and `ingest-contract`, container-first runtime flow, `make check|test|lint`, webhook ingest, reconcile runtime, and config-only MOLT verify references but no verify/cutover command seam yet.
- Inspected active task files directly: no active task has `<blocked_by>` or active `<priority>` tags. Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-09-verification-cutover/01-task-wrap-molt-verify-and-fail-on-log-detected-mismatches.md` as the most logical next task because it establishes the verification boundary required before cutover readiness, runbook documentation, and larger E2E verification stories. Next agent should use the `tdd` skill for that task and quit immediately after reading `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-09-verification-cutover/01-task-wrap-molt-verify-and-fail-on-log-detected-mismatches.md`; next I am reading the task body and any existing plan files to decide whether this turn is plan-only or execution.
- Need to obey Ralph flow exactly: if task/plan is still unverified, create or refine a plan and quit immediately; if it says `NOW EXECUTE`, run vertical TDD, then full checks (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`).

- The active task is still in planning state: the task file has no existing `TO BE VERIFIED` or `NOW EXECUTE` marker and there is no `_plans` directory yet.
- I am reading verification-related code and tests to design a concrete plan around the public interface, required red/green slices, and at least one boundary cleanup per the `improve-code-boundaries` skill.
- After that I will create the task plan file, mark it `NOW EXECUTE` if the design is coherent enough, record its path, and quit immediately per Ralph instructions.

- Read the runner CLI/config/investigation notes. There is config support for `verify.molt`, but no verify subcommand or wrapper yet; the natural seam is a new runner command plus a dedicated verification module.
- Key factual constraint from the investigation: MOLT can exit `0` and still report mismatches in JSON lines, especially via row-level `data` records and per-table `summary` counters.
- I am now reading existing Ralph plan-file conventions so the new plan directory, plan document, and task-file marker updates match the repo’s established workflow.

- Test style review shows fast contract tests are the right fit here: CLI-level `assert_cmd` coverage plus a small fake `molt` executable fixture can exercise process spawning, JSON-line parsing, and exit-status handling without relying on the real MOLT binary in the default lane.
- Before writing the plan I am checking the design docs/README for whether verification should be per-mapping or whole-config, because that choice determines the public CLI surface and the operator-facing result shape.
- Next edit will be the planning artifacts only: create the story-09 plan directory/file and add its path to the task file.

- Created the story-09 plan directory and added `.ralph/tasks/story-09-verification-cutover/01-task-wrap-molt-verify-and-fail-on-log-detected-mismatches_plans/2026-04-19-molt-verify-wrapper-plan.md`.
- Added the `<plan>` pointer to the active task file and marked the plan ready with `NOW EXECUTE` so the next turn can start execution immediately.
- Important design resolution captured in the plan: the current runner config has no Cockroach source connection details, so the planned public seam is `runner verify --config <path> --mapping <id> --source-url <cockroach-url> [--allow-tls-mode-disable]`; the next agent should execute vertical TDD slices from that plan and quit immediately if that interface proves wrong.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the saved state says the active story-09 verification task already has a plan ready with `NOW EXECUTE`; next I am reading the task file and plan to execute that design exactly unless the interface proves wrong.
- Immediate next step after any interruption: inspect the task/plan checklists, then start vertical TDD slices for the first planned behavior.

- Entered execution mode for story-09 and completed the first red/green TDD slice: added `verify_contract` with a fake `molt` executable, saw the expected red (`verify` subcommand missing), then implemented the minimum `runner verify` path.
- Added a dedicated `crates/runner/src/molt_verify/mod.rs` boundary so CLI dispatch stays shallow while the module owns request building, process execution, and basic summary/completion parsing.
- Next I should continue the vertical slices: add the mismatch contract where exit code `0` still fails, then tighten verdict reduction and argument/report behavior one slice at a time.

- Added verify contract coverage for clean match, structured mismatch failure, real-table argv scoping plus artifact persistence, incomplete-output failure with preserved raw logs, and the explicit TLS-disable passthrough.
- `make check` passed cleanly after the new `runner verify` boundary and test suite landed.
- Next I am running the remaining required lanes exactly as the task demands: `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, then I will do a final boundary review and update the Ralph task artifacts only if everything stays green.

- Completed the story-09 verify wrapper and the final boundary cleanup; the verification module now owns MOLT request building, structured log parsing, mismatch verdicts, artifact writes, and raw-log preservation on malformed/incomplete output.
- Verified completion twice after the last refactor: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed.
- Next I am updating Ralph state, running `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, then staging the full task scope for commit and push.
